reasoning: The hurricane season officially ends November 30, and the knowledge cutoff for this forecast is October 26. The peak of the Atlantic hurricane season (mid-August to mid-October) has passed, leaving only roughly one month of low-activity period. Historically, hurricanes with maximum sustained winds exceeding 160 mph are extremely rare; achieving such intensity in late October or November is nearly unprecedented (the 1932 Cuba hurricane being the primary historical outlier). With no >160 mph storm having occurred by late October, the likelihood of one forming and intensifying to that degree in the remaining weeks of the season is minimal. The probability is set to the lower limit of 0.02.
batch_queries: Will there be a stronger hurricane than Erin during the 2025 Atlantic hurricane season?
